era_context: Neo-Assyrian Empire under Shalmaneser III continues its dominance of the Near East. Levantine kingdoms remain tributary. Egypt is fragmented. Greek city-states are developing their characteristic political structures. Phoenician networks continue to expand. Chinese chronology for this period remains uncertain; traditional sources place the Spring and Autumn period later.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's continued Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III's annual military campaigns continued into the Levant, maintaining Assyrian tributary relationships and extracting resources from subject kingdoms. These campaigns are part of the documented pattern of Assyrian imperial expansion during this reign.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign and annual campaigns are well-documented. The specific year 807 BCE is assigned by chronological convention within his campaign sequence.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for 807 BCE. The year is historiographically assigned to Shalmaneser III's reign but has no documented distinctiveness.
historiographic_note: 807 BCE is poorly documented. The year is included for chronological continuity. No specific events are known with certainty. The historical record remains dominated by Assyrian sources and is heavily weighted toward military and political narratives.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's campaign sequence
